cards.back:
O(1) → O(log n) → O(n) → O(n log n) → O(n²) → O(2ⁿ) → O(n!)
Examples: hash lookup, binary search, single loop, merge sort, nested loops, recursive subsets, all permutations.
Drop constants. Drop smaller terms. Default = worst case.

cards.front: Two inputs of independent sizes — Big O?
cards.back: O(n + m). Don't collapse them into O(n) when n and m can vary independently (e.g. merging two arrays of length n and m).

cards.front: Big O for merge sort on n elements
cards.back:
O(n log n)
```
[8 7 5 1 | 6 4 3 2]
   ↓  split in half
[8 7][5 1][6 4][3 2]
   ↓  …until size 1
[8][7][5][1][6][4][3][2]
   ↑  merge, sorted
[1 2 3 4 5 6 7 8]
```
splits → log n levels
merge → n work per level
log n × n = O(n log n)

cards.front: Big O to generate all subsets of n items
cards.back:
O(2ⁿ)
```
each item → IN or OUT?  (2 choices)
  a      b      c
  2   ×  2   ×  2   = 2³ = 8

{} {a} {b} {c} {a,b} {a,c} {b,c} {a,b,c}
```
n yes/no switches → 2ⁿ
(arrange in order instead → n!)
